title: General Legal Aid - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > General Legal Aid 29 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Agricultural Laborers Migrants Native American Community Veteran/Military Personnel Issues CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES EUGENE REFUGEE AND IMMIGRANT SERVICES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides social services case management for asylum seekers, refugees, and others with immigration status for help with food, transportation, health services, OHP (Oregon Health Plan), RCA (Refugee Cash Assistance), SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program ), TANF (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families ), WIC (Women, Infants and Children), and other services. Also provides employment and legal services. 1464 W 6th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Varies according to program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 345-3628 1326 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD REFUGEE AND IMMIGRANT SERVICES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides social services case management for asylum seekers, refugees, and others with immigration status for help with food, transportation, health services, OHP (Oregon Health Plan), RCA (Refugee Cash Assistance), SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program ), TANF (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families ), WIC (Women, Infants and Children), and other services. Also provides employment and legal services. 1025 G Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Varies according to program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 345-3628 1326 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR NONPROFIT LEGAL SERVICES INC CENTER FOR NONPROFIT LEGAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides civil legal aid and advocacy, can not assist with criminal law cases. Organized into specialty units: Housing/Consumer Unit, Income Maintenance Unit, Employment/Individual Rights Unit (Immigration Law Project, Protecting Our Veterans, and Public Guardian Program), and Family Unit. 225 W Main Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Low-income households (125% of the Federal Poverty Level), age 60 and older, and veterans. Specialty units may have additional eligibility requirements. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-4pm (541) 779-7292 Get Directions More Details CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS OUTREACH OUTREACH CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Walk-in center providing referrals, food, clothing, DME, camping basic needs, and water for anyone experiencing homelessness or extremely low-income. Conducts outreach to veterans and non-veterans living in homeless camps by providing potable water, food, clothing, phone/fax services,gas vouchers (once per year), ordering DD214 documents for veterans, and facilitating connection to veterans services. 61510 S Highway 97 Suite 100 Bend, OR 977023706 Eligibility: Veterans who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness, who need to be screened for eligibility for programs and connection to services for housing, medical, employment and more. Street Outreach services are available for veteran and non-veteran homeless and low-income individuals and households. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3:45pm Email (541) 383-2793 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON ALBANY REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and representation for individuals who need assistance with routine civil legal matters, usually regarding housing, public benefits, or expungement of criminal records. Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: By appointment only (541) 926-8678 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON CENTRAL OREGON REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and representation for individuals who need assistance with routine civil legal matters, usually regarding housing, public benefits, or expungement of criminal records. Eligibility: Income must be at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level Hours: By appointment only (541) 385-6950 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON DOUGLAS COUNTY OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and representation for individuals who need assistance with routine civil legal matters, usually regarding housing, public benefits, or expungement of criminal records. Eligibility: Expungement: Individuals who have no criminal charges in the U.S. or foreign countries, no criminal convictions in the previous 3 years, no more than one conviction in the past 10 years, and are not currently on parole or probation. All other services: Must meet income guidelines. Prioritizes serving survivors of abuse or violent trauma and disabled individuals. Hours: By appointment only (800) 520-5292 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON FOREST GROVE FARMWORKER OFFICE FARMWORKER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help to migrant and seasonal farmworkers with employment, housing, unemployment, and tax issues. Most employment and housing issues are related to payment of wages, unauthorized pay deductions, poor living conditions in labor camps, retaliation for asserting legal rights, poor health and safety conditions on the job, pesticide problems, and discrimination. Also gives referrals to other kinds of legal programs. Eligibility: Agricultural (including seasonal) workers who have experienced civil legal problems in the State of Oregon Hours: By appointment only (503) 214-1384 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON KLAMATH FALLS REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and representation for individuals who need assistance with routine civil legal matters, usually regarding housing, public benefits, or expungement of criminal records. Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: By appointment only (541) 273-0533 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LINCOLN COUNTY OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and representation for individuals who need assistance with routine civil legal matters, usually regarding housing, public benefits, or expungement of criminal records. Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: By appointment only Email (541) 265-5305 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON NATIVE AMERICAN PROGRAM NATIVE AMERICAN PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides civil legal services to Indian tribes, Native organizations, and individual Native people for issues involving Indian law or tribal law or otherwise related to status as a tribal member. Does not handle domestic relations or housing matters (these types of cases are referred to local Legal Aid offices). Does not accept cases against Indian tribes. Current case priority categories include access to tribal courts, tribal court enhancement, protection of tribal sovereignty and self-government, Indian Child Welfare Act issues, tribal enrollment, issues involving trust land and resources, educational issues, health care issues, probate and estate planning, public benefits (where impacted by an applicant/recipient's Indian status), and domestic violence. Eligibility: Indian tribes and groups, and Native individuals. Must meet low-income eligibility requirements. Hours: By appointment only (503) 223-9483 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON PENDLETON REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and representation for individuals who need assistance with routine civil legal matters, usually regarding housing, public benefits, or expungement of criminal records. Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: By appointment only (541) 276-6685 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON PORTLAND REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and representation for individuals who need assistance with routine civil legal matters, usually regarding housing, public benefits, or expungement of criminal records. Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: By appointment only (503) 224-4086 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON SALEM FARMWORKER OFFICE FARMWORKER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help to migrant and seasonal farmworkers with employment, housing, unemployment, and tax issues. Most employment and housing issues are related to payment of wages, unauthorized pay deductions, poor living conditions in labor camps, retaliation for asserting legal rights, poor health and safety conditions on the job, pesticide problems, and discrimination. Also gives referrals to other kinds of legal programs. Eligibility: Agricultural (including seasonal) workers who have experienced civil legal problems in the State of Oregon Hours: By appointment only (503) 981-5291 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON SALEM REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and representation for individuals who need assistance with routine civil legal matters, usually regarding housing, public benefits, or expungement of criminal records. Eligibility: Income below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level. Must live in or have a legal issue in Marion or Polk Counties. Hours: By appointment only (503) 581-5265 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NOWIA UNETE CANNABIS WORKER RESILIENCE PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with filing claims for unpaid wages, legal assistance, mental health supports, temporary lodging, food boxes and vouchers, clothing, and relocation assistance. 607 W Main Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Individuals who are employed in the illegal marijuana industry Hours: Monday-Friday noon-3pm and 4pm-7pm Email (541) 840-9669 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON CRIME VICTIMS LAW CENTER LEGAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides victims of crime with legal help in asserting and enforcing their rights; assistance with social support services, including those that victims may need to effectively participate in the case against their offender and to help with recovery. Offers advocacy for victims of domestic violence in helping to get a protection order. In limited circumstances, can provide survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, and stalking with representation in civil protective order cases. Does not provide representation in domestic relation cases, including custody and dissolution. Does not assist with eviction assistance or landlord/tenant issues. Eligibility: Individuals who are victims of a crime who are current residents of Oregon or victims of a crime that was committed in the state of Oregon. The crime must have been filed by the court. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm by appointment only Email (503) 208-8160 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER COOS BAY REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with routine legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family, or elder law. Some locations may offer additional services. 490 N 2nd Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Walk in: Monday/Wednesday 1:30pm-4pm Telephone hours only: Tuesday/Thursday 1:30pm-4pm (541) 269-1226 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER GRANTS PASS REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with routine legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family, or elder law. Offers a senior law hotline. Some locations may offer additional services. 424 NW 6th Street Suite 102 Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Monday-Thursday 1pm-4pm (541) 471-3033 1-4 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER GRESHAM FARMWORKER OFFICE FARMWORKER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists with civil (non-criminal) employment issues such as unauthorized pay deductions, retaliation for asserting legal rights, poor health health and safety conditions, pesticide concerns, work related sexual harassment, and problems with work-related housing. Also provides information and referral for other types of legal programs. 138 NE 3rd Steet Suite 203 Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Individuals who are employed as agricultural workers including migrant and seasonal farmworkers Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 726-4381 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER HILLSBORO REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal advice and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family (focused on domestic violence), stalking, child support, employment, or elder law. Offers a tenant advice hotline. 230 NE Second Avenue Suite F Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Office: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon and 2pm-5pm, Friday 9am-noon; closed on 4th Friday of the month Tenant Hotline: Monday/Wednesday 9am-noon (503) 640-4115 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER LANE COUNTY LEGAL AID GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides general legal counseling and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with routine legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family, or elder law. Some locations may offer additional services. 101 E Broadway Suite 200 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Office hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 9am-11:45am and 12:45pm-5pm Intake for new clients: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 1pm-3pm (541) 485-1017 100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER MCMINNVILLE OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal advice and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family (focused on domestic violence), stalking, child support, employment, or elder law. Offers a tenant advice hotline. 117 NE 5th Street Suite B McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm Closed the 2nd Monday and 4th Wednesday of the month (503) 472-9561 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER ONTARIO REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with routine legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family, or elder law. Also provides legal assistance for survivors of domestic violence, including the obtainment of restraining orders. Some locations may offer additional services. 35 SE 5th Avenue Unit 1 Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-12:30pm and 1:30pm-5pm Email (541) 889-3121 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER PORTLAND REGIONAL OFFICE OREGON LAW HELP WEBSITE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Website offers legal information regarding various issues, including housing, family law, employment law, elder law, government benefits, and consumer protection. Provides links to legal service providers, resources, and a directory of legal aid providers. 621 SW Morrison Street Suite 1450 Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable (503) 224-4086 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER SALEM REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with routine legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family, or elder law. Offers a tenant advocate hotline.
